right-click an element and in the shortcut menu, click<span> </span><strong>Specification</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="6017ccfe-59b9-4fc2-ba8e-47840545b2f6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Double-click an element in the Containment tree or its symbol in the View Pane.</span></li><li data-uuid="5ea1845b-0116-45d4-bc51-092a66dbe991"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select an element in the Containment tree or its symbol on the View Pane and press Enter.</span></li><li data-uuid="64ac9549-fe01-4de9-ad71-eefea2dabb19"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click an element in the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Containment tree</span> or its symbol on the View Pane and click <strong>Specification </strong>in its shortcut menu.</span></li><li data-uuid="f4f29a44-6717-466c-ac28-87a6a6b18f82"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select an element in the Containment tree or its symbol on the View Pane, then in the main menu, click <strong>Window </strong>&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Specification</strong></span>.</span></li><li data-uuid="a6a3ade0-1bfa-413a-bb44-1ca49a0481a6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To open the Specification panel for a specific element displayed in the compartments section when <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the Specification panel is already open for another element, hover over the needed element and click the <strong>Navigate <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigate_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="12126292-c9e6-4dec-8c65-f416f2e5a0f9"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click an element cell in a tabular view and in its shortcut menu, click the <strong>Specification </strong>command. <span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><strong><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigating_to_an_element_specification_in_the_specification_pane.png" /></ac:image><br /></strong></span></span></span></li></ul><h3>Switching between Specification panel tabs</h3><p>Opening the Specification panel opens the Properties tab by default. </p><p>To switch between Specification panel tabs</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="886d7d1f-ea02-4938-a7d2-6671d9f522a8">Click the needed tab in the tab bar. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tabs_bar.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h2>Properties tab</h2><p>The Properties tab allows you to modify the element properties and manage related element compartments. </p><h3>Working with element properties via the Properties tab of the Specification panel</h3><h4>Modifying the declared name of the element via the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To modify the declared name of the element via the Specification panel</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1a33dbf5-fa88-4b1b-95b8-131af0138db0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="d62f1d4c-8d67-4338-bf34-e028c356f226"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section, p</span>lace the cursor on the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Declared Name </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">input field.</span></li><li data-uuid="f021f6dd-bcb2-4c06-9d4e-c4c981048f6c">Edit the declared name as needed.</li><li data-uuid="a9c4590b-0bd1-4bea-a67b-90d841c0f1ef">Click anywhere outside the edited input field for changes to take effect.</li></ol><h4>Modifying the declared short name of the element via the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To modify the declared short name of the element via the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9c612207-ce0f-4e90-aacb-91d3855f9279"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="1c4b3d50-4c91-40c6-8d42-085380166725"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section, p</span>lace the cursor on the <strong>Declared Short Name </strong><span>input field.</span></li><li data-uuid="79ffc62b-84eb-4e90-a943-3012060a888c">Edit the declared short name as needed.</li><li data-uuid="8dbfefd1-dde2-437c-9678-54db4aea370a">Click anywhere outside the edited input field for changes to take effect.</li></ol><h4>Modifying the feature type via the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To modify the feature type via the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2e2e0ea3-1209-4eb9-a049-7e4ef14b0202"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="9b765b39-dc89-4a3f-96a6-3d809631ce4d">Do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="ed312612-f0f8-4ddf-8b88-f2a1f0064f20">If the element has no applied types, do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="3358e817-ff4c-45a8-85f7-dfb95ced2d7e">Hover over the <strong>Type </strong>field, then click the <strong>Select Type </strong>button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select type.png" /></ac:image> to display the <strong>Type Selection </strong>menu. Click the needed type to apply it.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="871298b9-0efa-4faa-a2c1-81a89366bc23"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="f2055332-a884-4a2e-8330-d5ba3603dab6">The <strong>Type Selection</strong> menu displays both the applied types and those available for selection to replace the already specified type. </li><li data-uuid="3de9d6db-5a99-4bb3-93be-dadddb499f0f">The <strong>Type Selection </strong>menu has the <strong>Hide Uncommon Elements</strong> filter option enabled by default, which restricts the available selection to compatible types.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Type Selection Menu and Filter Options.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="e2bab27e-77ff-46bf-a79f-5f9a6a59045b">The field displays up to 10 elements. When more elements are available, click the label '<em>+&lt;number of available elements&gt; more</em>' to expand the field and view all elements.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Type field display limit.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="b75b6b0f-ca1b-4c45-86a1-987beaa19006">The ^ symbol indicates types inherited through redefinition, subsetting, reference subsetting, and feature typing.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inherited types.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="4a1e7cd7-358d-49f1-b2c3-c58a7f05504d">Drag and drop the typing element from the Containment tree on the <strong>Type </strong>field in the Specification panel.</li><li data-uuid="91100339-d218-460d-994c-cdaa075a7e9b">Select the typing element symbol in a view and use its Drag and Drop handler to drag and drop it on the <strong>Type </strong>field in the Specification panel. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34c60343-1664-4fa6-861a-0837cde6d60a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the type is already set, dragging and dropping the new type replaces the set type with the new one. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="08d67df4-4fae-436e-a9ef-76079db63d21">If the element already has applied types and you want to add additional ones, do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="33074453-5372-4cba-9cc3-9e8ddf052e58">Hover over the <strong>Type </strong>field, then click the <strong>Add Type</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select type.png" /></ac:image> to display the <strong>Type Selection </strong>menu. Click the needed type to apply it.</li><li data-uuid="2a985bc4-e975-4097-b6ec-f836b6e50ca1">Select the typing element symbol in a view and use its Drag and Drop handler to drag and drop it on the <strong>Type </strong>field in the Specification panel while holding the CTRL/Command key.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="8758bde1-6ab5-4ea5-8d56-5cb50a03e4eb">To remove applied type(s), do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="f5aaa0a3-324e-4b55-b56b-124012f62051">Hover over the <strong>Type </strong>field, then click the <strong>Select Type </strong>button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select type.png" /></ac:image> to display the <strong>Type Selection </strong>menu. In the menu, click an already applied type to remove it from the element. To remove all applied types, click <strong>&lt;UNSPECIFIED&gt;</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="f34b95aa-97bc-49c5-96f5-ab8c7bad4070">In the <strong>Type </strong>input field of the type you want to remove, click the <strong>Unspecify</strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_button.png" /></ac:image><strong> </strong>button.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="9301c9ea-c798-488f-8f26-51a56a1683d4">Click anywhere outside the edited input field for changes to take effect.</li></ol><h4>Modifying additional properties via the Specification panel</h4><p>Depending on the element, additional properties may be displayed in the Properties section of the Specification panel. These properties can be specified in the same way as for the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the feature type via the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[feature type property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ul><li data-uuid="68f565f9-7502-4345-89f6-dc3e7f37ac46"><strong>Result Expression</strong> - displays a r<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ead-only string of the constraint expression. </span></li><li data-uuid="58a23ae0-82a5-4fef-ba87-cfd3e820420c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Asserted Constraint</strong> - displays the asserted constraint's element name. See the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraints" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="3501a2fe-23c9-46b1-8627-8aa793b13886"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Assumed Constraint</strong> - displays the assumed constraint's element name. See the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraints" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="e7d8f6f5-4440-41ea-9249-745e266841e8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Required Constraint</strong> - displays the required constraint's element name. See the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraints" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="a0726bc9-cc65-47c2-a27e-9606379dc9ae"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Satisfied Requirement</strong> - displays the satisfied requirement's element name. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Satisfy requirement" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="9e794dfa-99c8-4d91-ba43-77bc3bcc3c4f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Verified Requirement</strong> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">displays the verified requirement's element name. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective" /></ac:link> page.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="f99cb408-3992-499a-ba44-1ed2ecdc039f"><strong>Exhibited State</strong> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">displays the exhibited state's element name. See the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exhibit state" /></ac:link><strong> </strong>page.</span></li><li data-uuid="fc103ccf-0a41-46e3-a4c9-bd0a465e2d73"><strong>Use Case Included</strong> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">displays the included use case's element name. See the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Include use case" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="2de58f78-ee11-42ec-97b6-76dc208db676"><strong>Performed Action</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> - displays the performed action's element name. See the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Perform action" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="e734a7e5-44f4-48a8-aebf-6995f14febc1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Referenced Rendering</strong> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">displays the referenced rendering's element name. See the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Rendering views basics" /></ac:link> page.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="614f6c87-632b-42b7-9805-624409f170f7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Event Occurrence</strong> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">displays the event occurrence's element name. See the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Occurrences" /></ac:link> page.</span></span></span></li><li data-uuid="d9fcd7d5-68ae-4dca-9f4a-7a0f8b6eafdc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Framed Concern</strong> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">displays the framed concern's element name. See the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Frame concern" /></ac:link> page.</span></span></span></li><li data-uuid="d4d2b5e6-2ead-44a3-aed3-7a997ff85d52"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Trigger </strong>- displays the transition's trigger element name. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="53058fd4-1c93-49da-8761-e6e7b757545f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Payload </strong>- displays the accept action's payload element name. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Accept action" /></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul><h4>Modifying the feature value via the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To modify the feature value via the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a6b93a12-d507-4486-9cdb-68f04cec611e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>In the Value subsection:<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49680761-927b-45eb-aa6e-128f7afffc84"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4abcbe6d-039a-4301-abcf-083326724bd9">You can only modify the field for already specified values. Otherwise, it is read-only. </li><li data-uuid="9e4b9b22-2e21-46aa-98e5-794d21559437">To delete the specified value, click the <strong>Delete</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_button.png" /></ac:image><strong> </strong>button next to the field.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li data-uuid="1e4592b9-2b4a-4ee2-a822-324bd956b7de">To modify the value modifier, click the first dropdown button and click the needed modifier.<br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Modifying_the_feature_value_of_the_element_via_the_Specification_panel.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="15a0f8e7-de92-403b-84ac-c4fb5386e7a9">A type-corresponding value editor is displayed:<ol><li data-uuid="52f160e0-7199-48c6-bbea-14d19151f751">For integer-typed attributes, click the up or down arrow buttons in the second field to modify the feature value. </li><li data-uuid="db977293-efaf-4762-9249-39e4ead97c4b">For string-typed attributes, input the needed text.</li><li data-uuid="11375f93-6df4-46d6-aa1c-4274afeb6252">For real-typed attributes, input the needed number.</li><li data-uuid="87d6d529-ba33-40b0-9d0f-942eaa107828">For boolean-typed attributes, select/deselect the checkbox to specify the needed value.</li><li data-uuid="751e6e85-8603-40c0-bab1-146e39784044">For enumeration-typed attributes, select the needed value from the dropdown list.</li><li data-uuid="58f7340e-834d-41d6-a5b8-737cfda494a2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For color-typed attributes, either click the color picker rectangle and pick the color, or input a 6 or 8-digit HEX color code.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="color picker.png" /></ac:image></span></li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="dc4c9c1f-2cc3-4de4-a8df-eccd676abc28">Click anywhere outside the edited field for changes to take effect.</li></ol><h4>Modifying the documentation of the element via the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To modify the documentation of the element via the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5ddac5d5-17a7-4f59-bba0-f271b1079a1a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="5c95db93-88a4-491a-ab19-fc135424077c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section, p</span>lace the cursor on the <strong>Documentation</strong><strong> </strong><span>input field.</span></li><li data-uuid="743d352e-030a-472f-9258-6abf55ce8635">Edit the declared name as needed.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="242e10eb-2613-4f76-a81d-4a0fa692f51d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To delete the specified documentation, click the <strong>Delete</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_button.png" /></ac:image><strong> </strong>button in the <strong>Documentation </strong>input field. Deleting the field content (using Backspace or Delete keys) does not delete the documentation but rather specifies it as empty.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="c556d8d7-5cd3-430f-8ba6-44ac06084ae4">Click anywhere outside the edited input field for changes to take effect.</li></ol><h4>Modifying the multiplicity of the element via the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To modify the multiplicity of the element via the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="929f31d0-9668-4154-aacd-56eafadf1298"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="2edef97a-de65-41f0-a56a-ef9815298771"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section, click the <span>input field</span> under the </span><strong>Multiplicity </strong><span>subsection </span><span>and do one of the following:</span><ol><li data-uuid="7f55d9dc-40c3-4274-bf01-5bad429cf0f3"><span>Select the needed multiplicity in the dropdown menu.</span></li><li data-uuid="119d42b0-25f6-47d3-86f7-b8bfb2a87c56"><span>Enter the needed multiplicity number.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="931f637d-69dd-4568-aee0-42d95a2e1210">Click anywhere outside the edited input field for changes to take effect.</li></ol><h4>Modifying the direction of the element via the Specification panel</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db28d088-7894-47b6-8977-f91760fd24ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Direction modification is only available for items, attributes, and refs.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To modify the direction of the element via the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1122dbb0-cb86-4a94-bff3-2e3a147921db"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="97fd79e5-358d-4dd9-99ff-eccdc104675e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section, click the<span> field</span> under the </span><strong>Direction </strong><span>subsection </span><span>and s</span>elect the needed direction in the dropdown menu.</li><li data-uuid="1bf4ec31-a1d9-4080-bf26-85f370ce94b4">Click anywhere outside the edited field for changes to take effect.</li></ol><h4>Creating an element via the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create an element via the Specification panel</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="188d2dae-e801-4560-81b1-4587fd1f1029"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="5044f87d-e655-43c5-9ff0-d7da29e3d0fc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears.</span></li><li data-uuid="444b9d7b-1d4a-4346-b3a3-08f90e09f12c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the needed element kind in the shortcut menu. The selected element is created in the appropriate compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_an_element_via_the_Specification_pane.png" /></ac:image></span></li></ol><h3>Working with elements via compartments in the Properties tab of the Specification panel</h3><h4>Creating an element via the compartment in the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create an element via the compartment in the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6b54f955-0c71-4e52-8c6e-aa05111f8792"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="7afd6db9-3355-4e87-b529-e202e72d0c8f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the appropriate compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create &lt;element name&gt;</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button (e.g., <em>Create attribute</em>). The element is created in the appropriate compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_an_element_via_the_compartment_in_the_Specification_pane.png" /></ac:image></span></li></ol><h4>Deleting an element via the compartment in the Specification panel</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To delete an element via the compartment in the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="05bf67db-6aca-4d4a-af45-49dc3cf481fc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="409c0bf5-ec9a-4bb1-af1a-0d054eb3f4cd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the appropriate compartment's section, hover over the element you want to delete.</span></li><li data-uuid="551a2ad0-9655-4e8c-94d7-ec9903d57442"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Delete <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> The element is deleted from the appropriate compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Deleting_an_element_via_the_compartment_in_the_Specification_pane.png" /></ac:image></span></li></ol><h4>Modifying element values via the compartment in the Specification panel</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87019873-5190-4338-b727-59be2039cdf5"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="c9188746-a780-4c0a-89b7-ddf22c322d75">This procedure is applicable only for string, integer, real, boolean, color, or enumeration-typed attributes and ref elements.<ul><li data-uuid="35a42e43-0b8f-40aa-b2d2-ca71cf8f01ca">If the modification is done for an inherited feature, the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">inherited feature is redefined with a new value.</span></li><li data-uuid="71013973-b110-46e1-a01c-b0b9f583f96b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For modifying other element values, <ac:link ac:anchor="Navigating elements via the Navigation tab"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[navigate to the element's Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, then see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the feature value via the Specification panel" /> procedure.</span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="bf1ebf50-ca82-4ae4-af79-d68e23b36e31">To delete the specified value, click the <strong>Delete</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_button.png" /></ac:image><strong> </strong>button next to the field.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To modify element values via the compartment in the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="52009103-fafe-423c-9266-47661d52e0cb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="1e47713f-493c-44c0-a6b5-2eb38211ac27"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the appropriate compartment's section, click the element whose value you want to modify. </span>A type-corresponding value editor is displayed:<ol><li data-uuid="c999cf20-69a2-4658-9829-cb3d5cb045c9">For integer-typed attributes, click the up or down arrow buttons in the second field to modify the feature value. </li><li data-uuid="d1287b64-3517-4f84-a828-93edb8c6809c">For string-typed attributes, input the needed text.</li><li data-uuid="edd4a394-ef41-48ba-9913-0038d0fe84b0">For real-typed attributes, input the needed number.</li><li data-uuid="f52c17ee-8f97-49f6-8e75-e69c74412408">For boolean-typed attributes, select/deselect the checkbox to specify the needed value.</li><li data-uuid="79a86dec-d386-4a3c-a66e-8b2a3af24fd3">For enumeration-typed attributes, select the needed value from the dropdown list.</li><li data-uuid="6aa1133a-7a81-4189-b60e-8aed665232ef"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For color-typed attributes, either click the color picker rectangle and pick the color, or input a 6 or 8-digit HEX color code.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="type-corresponding value editor.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="10b168f7-e3da-4824-bdce-2aef6d5a3c36">Click anywhere outside the edited field for changes to take effect.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ol><h4>Redefining inherited features via the compartment in the Specification panel</h4><p>The element features displayed in the compartments are grouped under labels indicating inherited and redefined features.</p><p>To redefine inherited features via the compartment in the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="48820c3e-ebf2-42f1-a6e9-e5d560312220"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following:</span><ol><li data-uuid="cfce2ae7-41cf-421f-bff8-fd7c783fe267"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d6be1781-801c-4a88-8260-d84230dc1e5c">To redefine a string, integer, real, boolean, color, or enumeration-typed inherited attributes or ref elements, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying element values via the compartment in the Specification panel" /> procedure.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Modifying element values via the compartment in the Specification panel.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="98fe38e2-5b79-4b41-9992-f530b63ee2fc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d6be1781-801c-4a88-8260-d84230dc1e5c">To redefine any inherited feature, do one of the following: </ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ol><li data-uuid="43ce1a4d-c5fe-4256-80f8-432dc6010a77"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d6be1781-801c-4a88-8260-d84230dc1e5c">H</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">over over the inherited feature you want to redefine and click the <span><strong>More </strong>button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="more button.png" /></ac:image>,<strong> </strong>then in the dropdown menu, <span>click <strong>Redefine</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="redefining inherited feature.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></span></li><li data-uuid="295289cd-06ff-419d-9bbb-66e4e673f78b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">H</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">over over the inherited feature you want to redefine and click the <span><strong>Navigate </strong>button <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigate_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>to open the Specification panel for the selected feature. At the top of the Specification panel, <span>click the <strong>Redefine</strong> button in the informational message.</span></span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c63ca14-7850-4f57-9b7c-18fa5d65de4b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The properties in the panel are read-only until redefinition is done to prevent modification of non-redefined property values.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Once done, you can modify the redefined feature's values via the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the feature value via the Specification panel" /> procedure.</span></span><br /></span></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Redefining_inherited_elements_via_the_compartment_in_the_Specification_panel.png" /></ac:image><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="706cb0f6-ee2c-4d53-8487-3beba4604566"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Redefining the inherited metadata feature without a set default value</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To redefine the inherited metadata feature without a set default value, c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick the <strong>Add</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button in the metadata compartment section's bar. In the submenu, hover over the needed metadata, and in its submenu, click the feature you want to redefine. The feature is redefined and displayed in the metadata compartment under the label of the applied metadata's name. NOTE: </span>The <strong>Add </strong>button is not displayed if all features are already redefined or the element inherits a feature with a set default value.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><h3>Using the Find in the Specification panel</h3><p>To use the Find functionality in the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9d5ac5e0-3dcc-4661-a834-4407e9352fdf"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and press Ctrl+F / Cmd+F. The <strong>Find </strong>input field is displayed at the top of the panel.</span></li><li data-uuid="607e0f7e-4c2c-4cfc-8fbe-d22df460d2d5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Enter the needed text in the <strong>Find </strong>field. Use the <strong>Next </strong>/ <strong>Previous </strong>buttons to select the findings.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Search functionality.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><h2>Relations tab</h2><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Relations tab allows you to access all incoming and outgoing relations of the selected element. The tab organizes relations into categories, such as Interface, Feature Typing, Connection, Exhibit, etc.</span></p><h3><span>Accessing relations via the Relations tab</span></h3><p>To access relations via the Relations tab</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="537dd6e9-4cbb-461b-a35c-43d2bc665770"><span>Click the Relations tab in the tabs bar.</span></li><li data-uuid="39af5478-de30-4fe1-aa0a-72bbec49ecad"><span>Hover over the needed relation and do one of the following:</span><ol><li data-uuid="4784eda6-ee39-4391-a090-12a96b792bc8"><span>Click the <strong>Navigate </strong>button <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigate_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong></span>to open the relation's Specification panel.</span></li><li data-uuid="6b6e4b43-ebcd-417c-991c-5cfe8669dcdd"><span>Click the </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_button.png" /></ac:image> button and in its submenu, click any of the available commands: <ol><li data-uuid="66568f51-0460-46ee-a26b-f65435d13e43"><strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="ffe4cc03-d370-4b11-b85f-db7a75e5cce8"><strong>Open in Textual Editor</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><h2>Navigation tab</h2><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The Navigation tab allows you to easily navigate from a selected element to its related elements based on their relations. The tab organizes related elements into categories, such as Subsetted Feature, Redefined Feature, Flow, Connection, Type, etc.</span></p><h3><span>Navigating elements via the Navigation tab</span></h3><p><span>To navigate elements via the Navigation tab</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="8d2a7505-73e7-4da4-9e89-48654c5ef894"><span>Click the Navigation tab in the tabs bar.</span></li><li data-uuid="f3b2ee0a-fc02-4252-9dcd-8bac5ee98d2c"><span>Hover over the needed element and do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span>Click the <strong>Navigate </strong>button <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigate_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong></span>to open the element's Specification panel.</span></li><li><span>Click the </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_button.png" /></ac:image> button and in its submenu, click any of the available commands:<ol><li data-uuid="97982d9b-5b75-4438-bb5c-29e9a14f8371"><strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="3537ef81-6a86-482b-9093-1a0d18c3aee0"><strong>Open in Textual Editor</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577248 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00690: Specification window

- page_id: `249577248`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577248/Specification+window
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-07-13T14:50:07.947+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Element management > Specification panel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Specification window allows you to review all properties of the specific element.

The Specification window is comprised of two columns:

- The Property column displays the name of the property.
- The Value column displays the corresponding value of the property.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Example of a Specification window for a *drone in any mission* part.

##### Opening the Specification window

To open the Specification window

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''].
2. In the Navigation bar of the Specification panel, click the **All Properties [IMAGE alt='' src='']** button. The Specification window opens.

##### Modifying element properties via the Specification window

You can modify string, number, and boolean values for properties in the Specification window

To modify element properties via the Specification window

1. .
2. Locate the property you want to modify.
3. In the Value column, do one of the following:
  1. To modify a string or number value, click the cell and type the needed value.
  2. To modify a boolean value, select the check box to set the value to true or deselect it to set the value to false . Read-only properties cannot be modified.
4. Click Close .

##### Filtering element properties and values

The Specification window filter allows you to filter element properties and values, allowing you to locate and display specific properties and their values in the Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Filtering element properties

To filter element properties

1. In the Specification window, right-click the Property column header and click Filter Column in the shortcut menu.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. Expand the Filter by Values section. Do one of the following:
    1. In the Values column, select the check boxes for the properties you want to display in the Specification window.
    2. Type the property name in the input field to narrow the search, select the Apply filter on refined values check box, and select the check boxes for the properties you want to display in the Specification window.
  2. Expand the Filter by Conditions section.
    1. Select the condition from the dropdown menu and type the keyword for the property you are looking for in the input field.
3. Click OK .

###### Filtering element property values

To filter element property values

1. In the Specification window, right-click the Value column header and click Filter Column in the shortcut menu.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. Expand the Filter by Values section. Do one of the following:
    1. In the Values column, select the check boxes for the property values you want to display in the Specification window.
    2. Type the property value in the input field to narrow the search, select the Apply filter on refined values check box, and select the check boxes for the property values you want to display in the Specification window.
  2. Expand the Filter by Conditions section.
    1. Select the condition from the dropdown menu and type the keyword for the property value you are looking for in the input field.
3. Click OK .

###### Resetting the filter

To reset the filter

1. Right-click the column header with the applied filter.
2. In the shortcut menu, do one of the following:
  1. Click Reset Column Filter .
  2. Click Filter Column , then Reset . Click OK .

###### Performing a quick element properties search

Instead of using filters, you can perform a quick element properties search using the Specification window's search bar.

To perform a quick element properties search

- In the Specification window, input the element property search keyword into the search bar at the top-left of the Specification window. The results are displayed in the Specification window.

##### Sorting element properties alphabetically

To sort element properties alphabetically

1. In the Specification window, click the Property column header. The Sorting icon is displayed in the header indicating the current sorting order. Sorting order types[IMAGE alt='' src=''] indicates that properties are sorted alphabetically in ascending order. This is the default mode.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] indicates that properties are sorted alphabetically in descending order.
2. Click the Property column header to change the sorting order.

##### Toggling semantic library elements' visibility in the Specification window

To toggle semantic library elements' visibility in the Specification window

1. .
2. Toggle the Display semantic library elements button at the top of the window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the <strong>Values</strong> column, select the check boxes for the property values you want to display in the Specification window.</li><li>Type the property value in the input field to narrow the search, select the <strong>Apply filter on refined values</strong> check box, and select the check boxes for the property values you want to display in the Specification window.</li></ol></li><li>Expand the <strong>Filter by Conditions</strong> section.<ol><li>Select the condition from the dropdown menu and type the keyword for the property value you are looking for in the input field.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Resetting the filter</h4><p>To reset the filter</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the column header with the applied filter.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, do one of the following:<ol><li>Click <strong>Reset Column Filter</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Filter Column</strong>, then <strong>Reset</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125633 space=SYSML2P version=26 -->
## PAGE 00691: Specifying a condition for a rule

- page_id: `296125633`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125633/Specifying+a+condition+for+a+rule
- version_number: 26
- version_date: `2026-07-10T16:32:50.151+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Symbol styles > Custom style sheets > Creating style sheets > Creating rules for a style sheet
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheets' space=''] and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating rules for a style sheet' space=''] for it, see the procedures on this page for specifying the condition for the rule.

##### Specifying a condition for a style sheet rule

To specify a condition for a style sheet rule via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheets' space=''] and a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating rules for a style sheet' space=''] for it.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] for the style sheet's rule part usage element.
3. Specify the condition in the Specification panel's condition compartment:
  1. Specify the needed condition in the Match by field. Click the field to view the dropdown menu with available conditions, then click the needed condition to specify it for the rule.
  2. Specify the condition values in the value field by doing one of the following:
    1. Specify values by dragging and dropping each needed element onto the input field.
    2. Hover over the input field and click the Add button to display the selection menu. Click the needed value(s) in the menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='condition value selection.png'] The value field name and its applicable values depend on the specified condition: ConditionValue Field NameDescriptionExplicit ElementsElementDrag and drop model elements from the Containment tree or a view to match these specific elements.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Element KindMetaclassDrag and drop a metaclass (e.g., a metaclass from the KerML library or a metadata definition from the SysML library) from the Containment tree to match elements of this type.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Metadata AnnotationMetadata DefinitionDrag and drop a metadata definition from the Containment tree or a view to match elements annotated with this metadata.[IMAGE alt='' src='']View TypeView DefinitionDrag and drop a view definition from the Containment tree to match the views of this type. The styles will apply to the symbols displayed in views defined by the specified view definition.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Custom ExpressionExpressionClick the **Open in Textual Editor**button and specify the expression in the Textual Editor.[IMAGE alt='' src='']The default expression is *true*. Replace it with an expression targeting the needed element. Use *element*to refer to the evaluated item.[IMAGE alt='' src='']Example in Textual Editorpart:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::FreeFormCondition{ 
calc:>>test{ 
(elementasKerML::Element).name=="vehicle" // targets element symbols named *vehicle* in all views (to which the style sheet is applied) 
} 
} 
}
4. You can now [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space=''] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Once you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating rules for a style sheet" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for it, see the procedures on this page for specifying the condition for the rule.</p><h3>Specifying a condition for a style sheet rule</h3><p>To specify a condition for a style sheet rule via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e9ea21a7-40d7-478b-890d-950152c1afb8"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating rules for a style sheet" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for it.</li><li data-uuid="c9dc903c-bebe-46c3-b8ca-fdc0d59b3d44"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the style sheet's <em>rule</em> part usage element.</li><li data-uuid="7588cd53-1a65-491b-b39d-309f586f9e44">Specify the condition in the Specification panel's <strong>condition </strong>compartment:<ol><li data-uuid="fec36ec4-733e-46d3-9f1b-d2910bee539c">Specify the needed condition in the <strong>Match by</strong> field. Click the field to view the dropdown menu with available conditions, then click the needed condition to specify it for the rule.</li><li data-uuid="035ff853-26f3-4643-a1a1-028e43db250b">Specify the condition values in the value field by doing one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="55e5e39c-4510-4596-8b6b-c3e47a117c2a">Specify values by dragging and dropping each needed element onto the input field.</li><li data-uuid="09b463d5-86f5-4128-8bb3-e8c81f5a16eb">Hover over the input field and click the <strong>Add </strong>button to display the selection menu. Click the needed value(s) in the menu.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="condition value selection.png" /></ac:image><br /><br />The value field name and its applicable values depend on the specified condition:<table class="wrapped" style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Condition</th><th>Value Field Name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Explicit Elements</td><td>Element</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Drag and drop model elements from the Containment tree or a view to match these specific elements.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Explicit Elements condition.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Element Kind</td><td>Metaclass</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Drag and drop a metaclass (e.g., a metaclass from the KerML library or a metadata definition from the SysML library) from the Containment tree to match elements of this type.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="element kind condition.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Metadata Annotation</td><td>Metadata Definition</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Drag and drop a metadata definition from the Containment tree or a view to match elements annotated with this metadata.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="metadata annotation condition.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>View Type</td><td>View Definition</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Drag and drop a view definition from the Containment tree to match the views of this type. The styles will apply to the symbols displayed in views defined by the specified view definition.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="view type condition.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Custom Expression</td><td>Expression</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the <strong>Open in Textual Editor </strong>button and specify the expression in the Textual Editor. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Custom expression Open in Textual Editor.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The default expression is <em>true</em>. Replace it with an expression targeting the needed element. Use <em>element </em>to refer to the evaluated item. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom expression condition.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b24caa5-1966-466a-89f4-095d65d4a96e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example in Textual Editor</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::FreeFormCondition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">test</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">name</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;vehicle&quot; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// targets element symbols named <em>vehicle</em> in all views (to which the style sheet is applied)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table></li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="f1fcc70b-caa0-4205-8fc8-558f7c38d5c4">You can now <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying styles for a rule" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify styles for the rule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and/or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assessing layout-sensitive changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[assess layout-sensitive changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048007 space=SYSML2P version=16 -->
## PAGE 00692: Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective

- page_id: `258048007`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/258048007/Specifying+requirement+verification+usages+for+verification+case+objective
- version_number: 16
- version_date: `2026-02-17T21:28:16.372+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Analysis & Verification > Verification case
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In addition to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement constraints' space='']usages allowed in any requirement usage, the objective of a verification case may also have**requirement verification usages**, which indicate the requirements to be verified by the verification case. Since the requirement verification usage is a subrequirement of the objective, it is automatically considered a required constraint of its containing objective.

There are two ways a requirement verification usage can be specified for a verification case objective:

50%

- by referencing the requirement usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the verify keyword (unnamed element) or the verify requirement keyword (named element) . [ATTACHMENT filename='Specifying requirement usages defined elsewhere in the model as requirement verification usages for verification case objective.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned requirement usage, i.e., the verified requirement is the owned requirement usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the verify requirement keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the owned verify requirement usage itself as the verified requirement.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 

##### Specifying requirement usages defined elsewhere in the model as requirement verification usages for verification case objective

###### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Textual Editor

To specify requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the verification case objective's body, do one of the following :
  1. For an unnamed requirement verification usage, declare the verify keyword followed by the name of the requirement you want to reference.
  2. For a named requirement verification usage, declare the verify requirement keyword, name the new usage, and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''] the requirement you want to reference.
2. (Optional) Specify the requirement in the element's body.
3. Click the Synchronize button. requirementdefVehicleMassRequirement{ 
//... 
}requirementvehicleMassRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
inmassActual:>ISQ::mass=vehicle.mass; 
doc/* The vehicle mass shall be less than or equal to 2500 kg. */ 
requireconstraint{ 
massActual<=2500[SI::kg] 
} 
}// step 1.a.verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyvehicleMassRequirement;// unnamed requirement verification usage reference subsets another requirement usage 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
}// step 1.bverificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyrequirementvehicleMassRequirementForTest::>vehicleMassRequirement;// named requirement verification usage reference subsets another requirement usage 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
}

###### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To specify requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a requirement element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - a verification element symbol that you want to verify the requirement by referencing it using the Create Verification Requirement of Objective command.
  - a verification usage symbol (keyword *verify requirement*) that you want to verify by referencing it using the **Set Verified Requirement** command.

###### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Specification panel

To specify requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a requirement element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the **Verified Requirement** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the verification usage symbol element (keyword *verify requirement*) . The appropriate requirement is specified for the element.

###### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the view palette

To specify requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Cases group, click verify .
2. Do one of the following:
  1. To specify an existing requirement as a requirement verification usage for the verification case objective, inthe view pane, click on the verification element symbol, then on the requirement element symbol.
  2. To specify a new requirement as a requirement verification usage for the verification case objective, in the view pane, click on the verification element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol. The verify path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the smart manipulator in a view

To specify requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view , do one of the following:
  - To specify an existing requirement as a requirement verification usage for the verification case objective, select the verification element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the verify command. Click on the requirement element symbol.
  - To specify a new requirement as a requirement verification usage for the verification case objective, select the verification element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the verify command. Click elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol . The verify path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objectivevia the Create Relation command

To specify requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click on the supplier (target) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Incoming , click verify . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the client (source). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the client (source) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the client (source) from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

##### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the owned verify requirement usage itself as the verified requirement

###### Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Textual Editor2Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Textual Editor

To specify requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the verification case objective's body, declare the verify requirement keyword, specify the element name, then the requirement body.
2. Click the Synchronize button. verificationdefVehicleMassTest{ 
subjecttestVehicle:Vehicle; 
objectivevehicleMassVerificationObjective{ 
verifyrequirementvehicleMassRequirement{// new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usage 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
inmassActual:>ISQ::mass=vehicle.mass; 
doc/* The vehicle mass shall be less than or equal to 2500 kg. */ 
requireconstraint{ 
massActual<=2500[SI::kg] 
} 
} 
} 
returnverdict:VerificationCases::VerdictKind; 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

shall be less than or equal to 2500 kg. */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">require</span><span /></ac:link> and drop it on:<ul><li data-uuid="3a3072f2-48eb-44b1-8a29-b7f67c9f9d08">a verification element symbol that you want to verify the requirement by referencing it using the <strong Requirement </strong></span>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the verification usage symbol element <span>(keyword <em>verify requirement</em>)</span>. <span><br /></span>The appropriate requirement is specified for the element.</li></ul><h4>Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the view palette</h4><p><span data-uuid="64dac03a-b6cd-4c10-ad1a-7f9bee5589e1">In the view palette, under the Cases group, click <strong>verify</strong>.<strong> </strong></li><li verification case objective, i</span>n the view pane, click on the verification element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol. <br /></span>The <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">verify</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> path is drawn between the element symbols.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Specifying requirement verification usages verification usage for the verification case objective, </span>select the verification element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the<strong><span> verify </span></strong>command. Click on the requirement element symbol.</li><li data-uuid="8f932eb9-885e-4a61-a5c9-b1c73f7f07c3"><span>To specify a new requirement as a requirement verification usage for the verification case objective, </span>select the verification element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the<strong><span> verify </span></strong>command. Click <span>elsewhere in the view pane to create a requirement element symbol</span>.<br />The <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">verify</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> path is drawn between the element symbols.</span></li></ul></li></ul><h4><span>Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective </span><span>via the Create Relation command</span></h4><p>To specify requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Create Relation command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ab788f23-eb50-42b3-85c8-69203b254e88">Right-click on the supplier (target) element.</li><li data-uuid="c9755399-cb4d-41df-88e2-fd3417abb2a3">In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relation</strong>, then in the submenu, under <strong>Incoming</strong>, click <strong>verify</strong>. The <strong>Create... </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="33bdd893-206f-4f36-b058-9f43242c94c5">In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the client (source). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the client (source) and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the client (source) from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Create... </strong>dialog.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="6960fe0c-11a6-4f87-96f3-8c69730a3cbb">In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h3>Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the owned verify requirement usage itself as the verified requirement</h3><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37999d9c-7fa3-4749-a838-11eca15ee52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Textual Editor2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify requirement verification usages for verification case objective via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1f3e50ef-9b73-4a62-b017-963ac14c7df5">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the verification case objective's body, declare the <em>verify requirement<span> </span></em>keyword, specify the element name,<span> </span>then the requirement body.</li><li data-uuid="ba8df09f-6b96-4873-b170-ba1edc64724c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the </span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="091acc46-a8a5-4212-b58b-4a4c5f651662"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span       </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">verify</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new verification case objective-owned requirement verification usage</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">massActual</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ISQ::mass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mass</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">doc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* The vehicle mass shall be less than or equal to 2500 kg. */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">require</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">constraint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">SI::kg</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">]</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span
