Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateRequirementUsageSubjectParameterPosition</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The subject must be the first parameter. Current first parameter: $currentFirstParameter.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateForLoopActionUsageLoopVariable</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The first owned feature must be a reference usage. Current first owned feature: $currentFirstParameter.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateDefinitionVariationIsAbstract</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A variation must be abstract.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateEnumerationDefinitionIsVariation</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be a variation.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateExposeOwningNamespace</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by a view usage.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateExposeVisibility</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must have protected visibility.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateExposeIsImportAll</p></td><td>An expose relationship always imports all elements, regardless of visibility.</td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFramedConcernMembershipConstraintKind</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A framed concern membership must have a 'requirement' kind. </span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateRequirementVerificationMembershipKind</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A requirement verification membership must have a 'requirement' kind.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateOccurrenceUsageIsPortion</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An occurrence with a portion kind must be a portion.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateVariantMembershipOwningNamespace</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The owning namespace of a variant membership must be a variation-point definition or usage. Current namespace: $namespace.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTransitionFeatureMembershipOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The owning type of a transition feature membership must be a transition usage. Current owning type: $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateDecisionNodeIncomingSuccessions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A decide may have at most one incoming succession. Currently has: $successions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateForkNodeIncomingSuccessions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A fork may have at most one incoming succession. Currently has: $successions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateJoinNodeOutgoingSuccessions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A join may have at most one outgoing succession. Currently has: $successions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateMergeNodeOutgoingSuccessions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A merge may have at most one outgoing succession. Currently has: $successions. </span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateControlNodeOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by an action.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssignmentActionUsage</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The target of an assignment action must be able to have time-varying values. Current target: $target.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssignmentActionUsageReferent</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An assignment action must reference a feature as its target. Current target: $referent.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateConjugatedPortDefinitionOriginalPortDefinition</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A conjugated port definition must reference the same original port definition as its port conjugator. Current conjugator: $portConjugator.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateStateUsageParallelSubactions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A parallel state cannot own successions or transitions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateStateDefinitionParallelSubactions</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A parallel state cannot own successions or transitions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTransitionFeatureMembershipEffectAction</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A transition feature whose kind is 'effect' must be an action usage.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTransitionFeatureMembershipGuardExpression</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A transition feature whose kind is 'guard' must be an expression whose result is Boolean with multiplicity 1..1. </span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTransitionFeatureMembershipTriggerAction</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A transition feature whose kind is 'trigger' must be an accept action usage.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateObjectiveMembershipIsComposite</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be composite.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateOccurrenceUsageIndividualDefinition</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An occurrence usage must be typed by at most one individual occurrence definition. Currently typed by: $IndividualDefinitions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateOccurrenceUsageIndividualUsage</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An individual occurrence usage must be typed by an individual occurrence definition. Currently typed by: $definitions.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateOccurrenceUsagePortionKind</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An occurrence usage with a portion kind must be owned by an occurrence definition or another occurrence usage. Current owning type is $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateCrossSubsettingCrossedFeature</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The crossed feature must define exactly two chaining features, and their order must match the crossed end features. Current chaining features: $chainingFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateRedefinitionDirectionConformance</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Direction must conform to the direction of the redefined feature. Currently redefined feature: $redefinedFeature.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>validateRedefinitionFeaturingTypes</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A package-level feature cannot be redefined. Currently redefined feature: $redefinedFeature. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d10dfc27-b3de-40e8-bed7-a60eb27906ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When a package-level feature is redefined.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="2">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A feature cannot redefine another feature within the same type. Currently redefined feature: $redefinedFeatures.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fb6a449-7548-4088-b232-4eb0a094f422"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When the redefining and the redefined features are owned by the same type.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateSubsettingFeaturingTypes</span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The feature cannot access a subsetted feature. Tip: use dot notation for nesting. Non-accessible feature: $subsettedFeature.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateRedefinitionEndConformance</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-end feature cannot redefine an end feature. Currently redefined end feature: $redefinedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateSubsettingUniquenessConformance</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-unique feature cannot subset a unique feature. Currently subsetted unique feature: $subsettedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateSubsettingConstantConformance</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A variable feature cannot subset a constant feature. Currently subsetted constant feature: $subsetedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFeatureValueOverriding</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cannot override a non-default value - the redefined feature already has a non-default value. Redefined feature(s): $redefinedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTypeOwnedMultiplicity</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one multiplicity is allowed. Current multiplicities: $multiplicityRange.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFlowPayloadFeature</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one payload is allowed. Current payloads: $payloadFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFeatureValueOverriding</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Cannot override a non-default value – the redefined feature already has a non-default value. Redefined feature(s): $redefinedFeatures.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateReturnParameterMembershipOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by a calculation or a constraint. Currently owned by: $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateResultExpressionMembershipOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by a calculation or a constraint. Currently owned by: $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFlowEndOwningType</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be owned by a flow. Currently owned by: $owningType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateStructureSpecialization</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A structure must not specialize a behavior. Currently specialized behavior(s): $specializedBehavior.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateBehaviorSpecialization</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A behavior must not specialize a structure. Currently specialized structure(s): $specializedStructure.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateSpecificationSpecificNotConjugated</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A conjugated type cannot specialize another type. Currently specialized type(s): $generalType.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateTypeAtMostOneConjugator</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one conjugation relationship is allowed. Current conjugation relationship(s): $ConjugationRelationships.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>validatePortDefinitionConjugatedPortDefinition</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned conjugated port definition is allowed. Currently owned conjugated port definition(s): $ConjugationPortDefinitions.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c9032f3-b794-42f6-9492-4a05a1609f82"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When there is more than 1 owned conjugated part definition.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="2">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own a conjugated port definition.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6480dffd-1f27-46ee-aeab-93d53287bc9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When there are no owned conjugated port definitions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>validateExpressionResultExpressionMembership</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one expression is allowed. Current expressions: $expressionList.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFunctionResultExpressionMembership</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one expression is allowed. Current expressions: $expressionList.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateFeatureValueIsInitial</p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-variable feature cannot have an initial feature value.</span></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p>validateFlowEndNestedFeature</p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one owned feature is allowed. Current owned features: $ownedFeatureList.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b157907-d767-482c-be88-a8d6f117762c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When there is more than one owned feature.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="2">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own one feature.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab2ba156-846b-416a-ae42-3cb5b3d7b1b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When there are no owned features.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssociationBinarySpecialization</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An association with more than two ends must not specialize $binaryLink.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateConnectorBinarySpecialization</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A connector with more than two ends must not specialize $binaryLink.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssociationRelatedTypes</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Not abstract association must have at least two related types.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateConnectorRelatedFeatures</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Not abstract connector must have at least two related features.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateAssociationEndTypes</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An end feature must have exactly one type. Current types: $types.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p>validateBindingConnectorIsBinary</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must have exactly two related features. Current related features: $ends.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateEndFeatureMembershipIsEnd</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be an end feature.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFlowEndIsEnd</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must be an end feature.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureEndNoDirection</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An end feature must not have a direction.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureEndIsConstant</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An end feature that is variable must also be constant.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureEndNotDerivedAbstractCompositeOrPortion</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An end feature must not be derived, abstract, composite, or have a portion kind value.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateImportTopLevelVisibility</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A top-level import must have private visibility.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateExpressionResultParameterMembership</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own exactly one result. Currently owned results: $results.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ValidateFunctionResultParameterMembership</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must own exactly one result. Currently owned results: $results.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeaturePortionNotVariable</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A feature with portion kind must not be variable.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateClassifierMultiplicityDomain</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A multiplicity owned by a classifier must not have any featuring types.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureMultiplicityDomain</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A multiplicity owned by a feature must have a featuring type the same as the feature itself.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateMultiplicityRangeBounds</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The lower bound (if any) and upper bound expressions must be the first owned members. Currently owned members before bounds: $ownedMembers.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateMultiplicityRangeBoundResultTypes</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Multiplicity range bounds must be integer-valued and non-negative when evaluable.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateDataTypeSpecialization</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A data type must not specialize a class or an association. Currently specialized class(es) and/or association(s): $specializedClassAssociation.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateClassSpecialization</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A classifier must not specialize a data type or an association. Currently specialized data type(s) and/or association(s): $specializedDataTypeAssociation.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureChainingFeatureConformance</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">$chainingFeature must be featured within $previousChainingFeature.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureChainingFeatureNotOne</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A feature must have either no chaining features or more than one.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureChainingFeaturesNotSelf</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A feature cannot be one of its own chaining features.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureConstantIsVariable</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A constant feature must be variable.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureIsVariable</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A variable feature must directly or indirectly specialize Occurrences::Occurrence.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureValueIsInitial</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-variable feature cannot have an initial feature value.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateCollectExpressionOperator</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The operator must be 'collect'. Current operator: '$operator'.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureChainExpressionOperator</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The operator must be '.'. Current operator: '$operator'.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateIndexExpressionOperator</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The operator must be '#'. Current operator: '$operator'.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateSelectExpressionOperator</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The operator must be 'select'. Current operator: '$operator'.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateInstantiationExpressionInstantiatedType</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An instantiation expression must have an instantiated type.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateParameterMembershipParameterDirection</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The member of a parameter membership must have the '$expectedDirection' direction. Current direction: '$memberDirection'.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateParameterMembershipOwningType</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The owning type of a parameter membership must be a behavior, step, or result parameter of a constructor expression. Current owning type: $owningType.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateInstantiationExpressionResult</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An expression must own its result parameter.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureReferenceExpressionResult</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An expression must own its result parameter.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateAnnotationAnnotatedElementOwnership</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An annotation must own its annotating element if and only if it is owned by the annotated element.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateAnnotationAnnotatingElement</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An annotation must own its annotating element or be owned by one, but not both.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateConstructorExpressionNoDuplicateFeatureRedefinition</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A constructor expression must not redefine the same feature more than once. Current features redefined more than once: $redefinedFeatures.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateConstructorExpressionOwnedFeatures</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A constructor expression must not own any features other than its result. Currently owned features other than the result: $ownedFeatures.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateElementFilterMembershipConditionIsBoolean</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A condition result must directly or indirectly specialize ScalarValues::Boolean.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureReferenceExpressionReferentIsFeature</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The first non-parameter owned member must be a feature.<br />Current first owned member: $firstMember.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateInvocationExpressionInstantiatedType</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The instantiated type of an invocation expression must be a behavior or a feature typed by exactly one behavior. Current instantiated type(s): $instantiatedTypes.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateInvocationExpressionOwnedFeatures</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">All owned features of an invocation expression, other than its result, must have the 'in' direction. Currently owned features with other directions: $ownedFeaturesWithoutInDirection.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateInvocationExpressionParameterRedefinition</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Each input parameter of an invocation expression must redefine exactly one input parameter of its instantiated type. Input parameters with zero or multiple redefinitions: $inputParameters.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureEndMultiplicity</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">End feature must have a multiplicity of [1].</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureOwnedReferenceSubsetting</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one reference subsetting (::&gt;) is allowed. Current reference subsetting: $referenceSubsettedList.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureOwnedCrossSubsetting</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Only one cross subsetting (=&gt;) is allowed. Current cross subsettings: $crossSubsettedList.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateRedefinitionEndConformance</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-end feature cannot redefine an end feature. Currently redefined end feature: $redefinedFeatures.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateSubsettingUniquenessConformance</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A non-unique feature cannot subset a unique feature. Currently subsetted unique feature: $subsettedFeatures.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateSubsettingConstantConformance</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A variable feature cannot subset a constant feature. Currently subsetted constant feature: $subsetedFeatures.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateInvocationExpressionInstantiatedType</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The instantiated type of an invocation expression must be a behavior or a feature typed by exactly one behavior. Current instantiated type(s): $instantiatedTypes.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateInvocationExpressionOwnedFeatures</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">All owned features of an invocation expression, other than its result, must have the 'in' direction. Currently owned features with other directions: $ownedFeaturesWithoutInDirection.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateInvocationExpressionParameterRedefinition</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Each input parameter of an invocation expression must redefine exactly one input parameter of its instantiated type. Input parameters with zero or multiple redefinitions: $inputParameters.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateInvocationExpressionNoDuplicateParameterRedefinition</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An invocation expression must not have multiple owned features redefining the same feature of its instantiated type. Current duplicate redefinitions: $duplicateRedefinedFeatures redefines $redefinedFeature.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateMetadataFeatureBody</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A metadata feature must be unnamed, redefine exactly one feature, have only model-level evaluable values, and only own features with the same restrictions. Invalid feature(s) of metadata: $InvalidFeaturesOfMetadata.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateMetadataFeatureAnnotatedElement</span></p><br /><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must annotate an element whose metaclass is $AllowedMetaclasses.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecf024fc-5a5f-4844-9779-6e88d7be1c2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There is only one ref element that subsets/redefines annotatedElement in the metadata definition.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="3">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Must annotate an element whose metaclass is one of: $AllowedMetaclasses.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a036f41-69db-46be-9e59-3a0e855d4078"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are multiple ref elements that subset annotatedElement in the metadata definition.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Non-matching annotated element(s): $NonMatchingElement.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e3a4e60-fb09-4326-9b67-a4dd57d27a61"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The same metadata usage annotates multiple elements.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateMetadataAccessExpressionReferencedElement</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A metadata access expression must have at least one member that is not owned by a feature membership.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateMetadataFeatureMetaclass</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A metadata feature must be typed by exactly one metaclass. Currently typed by: $types.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateMetadataFeatureMetaclassNotAbstract</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">A metadata feature must be typed by a non-abstract metaclass. Currently typed by: $type.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateCrossSubsettingCrossingFeature</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The crossing feature must be an end feature owned by a type with at least two end features.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureCrossFeatureSpecialization</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The owned cross feature must specialize the cross features of all redefined features.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureCrossFeatureType</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The owned cross feature must have the same types as this feature.<br />Feature type(s): $featureType. Cross feature type(s): $crossFeatureTypes.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">validateFeatureChainExpressionConformance</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The target feature $targetFeature must be featured within $argumentResult.</span></p></div></td><td>Error</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180945 space=SYSML2P version=13 -->
## PAGE 00717: SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition

- page_id: `314180945`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314180945/SysML+v1+to+SysML+v2+transition
- version_number: 13
- version_date: `2026-06-25T10:53:02.839+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section provides guidance for transitioning SysML v1 projects to SysML v2. Existing SysML v1 models can be transformed into SysML v2 models using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Prerequisites' space='']**SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration**Plugin.

The transition process consists of several stages, including organizational preparation, model selection, model pre-processing, transformation, post-processing, and validation. Detailed guidance for each stage is provided in the corresponding subpages of this section:

true

To help you understand the overall migration workflow, you can download a sample project [ATTACHMENT filename='DS SysML v1 to v2 transition.mdzip'], containing diagrams that illustrate the transition process. Each diagram is explained in detail throughout the related subpages, providing additional context and guidance for every step of the transition.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="isSelectedEnd"><span>This section provides guidance for transitioning SysML v1 projects to SysML v2. Existing SysML v1 models can be transformed into SysML v2 models using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Prerequisites" /><ac:link-body><strong>SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration </strong>Plugin</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p class="isSelectedEnd"><span>The transition process consists of several stages, including organizational preparation, model selection, model pre-processing, transformation, post-processing, and validation. Detailed guidance for each stage is provided in the corresponding subpages of this section:</span></p><p class="isSelectedEnd"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="aba0f343-211f-4239-a6f0-d5975452c592"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><span>To help you understand the overall migration workflow, you can download a sample project <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="DS SysML v1 to v2 transition.mdzip" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v1 to SysML v2 Transition.mdzip]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, containing diagrams that illustrate the transition process. Each diagram is explained in detail throughout the related subpages, providing additional context and guidance for every step of the transition.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180906 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00718: SysML v1 to v2 model conversion

- page_id: `314180906`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314180906/SysML+v1+to+v2+model+conversion
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:22:51.540+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Overview

This activity diagram provides a high-level overview of the SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition process based on the OMG SysML v1 to SysML v2 Transition Guidelines, extended with additional activities identified from practical migration experience.

The diagram also highlights two migration strategies:

- One-off migration , where the source SysML v1 model is no longer maintained after transformation and further development continues only in SysML v2.
- Incremental migration , where the SysML v1 model continues to evolve and migration is performed in multiple stages or model subsets.

##### Model transition

The transition process consists of two major stages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preparing the organization for transition' space=''] .
- Deployment of MBSE with SysML v2 to selected programs.

##### Model conversion

Once the organization is prepared, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting SysML v1 models for migration' space=''] are migrated through a model conversion process consisting of four consecutive stages:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pre-processing the SysML v1 model' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transforming the SysML v1 model to v2' space=''] .
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post-processing the SysML v2 model' space=''] .
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validating the transformed model' space=''] .

The conversion process is repeated for each selected SysML v1 project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

SysML v2 adoption]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="27de88c9-4c3a-4fbe-a220-c91edfd2ba81">Deployment of MBSE with SysML v2 to selected programs.</li></ul><h3>Model <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314181005 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00719: SysML v1 to v2 transition context

- page_id: `314181005`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314181005/SysML+v1+to+v2+transition+context
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:21:24.740+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Overview

This use case diagram provides a high-level view of the SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition process, identifying the primary stakeholders and tools involved in the migration effort.

It establishes the overall context for the transition initiative before detailing the individual migration activities described in subsequent diagrams.

##### Purpose

The purpose of the transition process is to enable organizations to systematically migrate SysML v1 projects to SysML v2 while preserving model intent, minimizing migration risks, and enabling adoption of SysML v2 modeling capabilities and practices.

##### Participants

The transition process involves multiple stakeholder groups that collaborate throughout the migration lifecycle:

- Inter-disciplinary cross-functional team. Responsible for establishing organizational transition strategies, defining migration goals, securing resources, coordinating stakeholders, and overseeing transition execution.
- Systems engineer. Responsible for evaluating migration readiness, selecting migration candidates, defining transformation configurations, performing model preparation activities, and validating transformed models.
- IT engineer. Responsible for deploying and maintaining the technical infrastructure required to support SysML v2 adoption, including modeling environments, repositories, and supporting services.
- Software developer. Responsible for adapting and maintaining custom extensions, integrations, reports, scripts, and other software components required for the SysML v2 environment .
- Partners and third parties. External organizations that may participate in migration activities, tool development, methodology development, training, or integration support.
- Tooling environment. The transition process may involve several independent tools:
- SysML v1 tool. The source modeling environment containing the existing SysML v1 projects that will be migrated.
- SysML v2 tool. The target modeling environment where transformed SysML v2 models are created, maintained, and evolved.
- SysML v1-to-v2 transformation tool. A dedicated transformation solution responsible for converting SysML v1 models into SysML v2 representations. Depending on the implementation, this capability may be integrated into modeling tools or provided as a standalone transformation service.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

v2 transition process, identifying the primary stakeholders and tools involved in the migration effort.</p><p>It establishes the overall context for the transition initiative before detailing the configurations, performing model preparation activities, and validating transformed models.</li><li data-uuid="0a654d90-75cb-44a3-98ab-114c1e1a7504"><strong>IT engineer. </strong>Responsible for deploying and maintaining the technical infrastructure required to support SysML v2 adoption, including modeling environments, repositories, and supporting services.</li><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576493 space=SYSML2P version=18 -->
## PAGE 00720: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation

- page_id: `249576493`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576493/SysML+v2+Plugin+Documentation
- version_number: 18
- version_date: `2026-06-09T07:55:09.653+02:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

SysML v2 is a next-generation systems modeling language. Compared to SysML v1, SysML v2 intends to provide improved precision, expressiveness, and usability of the modeling language.

To fully take advantage of SysML v2, three primary plugins are available for you:

- The SysML v2 Plugin provides core SysML v2 features and includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, enabling you to model using both textual and graphical notations.
- The [SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2EP/?contextKey=sysml-v2-evaluation-plugin-documentation&version=latest) allows you to conduct static evaluations of SysML v2 models .
- The [SysML v2 Simulation Plugin](https://docs.nomagic.com/SYSML2SP/?contextKey=sysml-v2-simulation-plugin-documentation&version=latest) allows you to execute SysML v2 models.

For more information, please see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Prerequisites' space=''] page.

For your convenience, you can download the**SysML v2 Quick Sheet**as a concise reference to the SysML v2 language on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Quick Sheet' space=''] page.

The following topics explain how to install and use the SysML v2 Plugin:

true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577744 space=SYSML2P version=28 -->
## PAGE 00722: Tabular view columns

- page_id: `249577744`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577744/Tabular+view+columns
- version_number: 28
- version_date: `2026-05-06T22:30:14.975+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views > Tabular views > Modeling tabular view columns
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Tabular views contain columns used to represent specific information about the exposed elements. See the information on this page to learn about table columns before you start [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling tabular view columns' space=''].

##### Columns in tabular view definitions and usages

Tabular views and their definitions can differ in the table columns they contain:

- Tabular view definitions:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] come with predefined columns.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom tabular view definitions' space=''] contain only the columns you specify.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] inherit columns from the view definitions they are defined by. You can also specify custom columns for them.

##### Column kinds

Currently, there are 2 model-based table column kinds available for modeling. The columns you create for a tabular view usage or definition must be defined by one of these column definitions, depending on the column's purpose. The table below lists:

- Column kinds and their descriptions.
- Relevant information regarding cell type according to column kind.
  - The cell type enables correct data representation and correct cell value type (e.g., String, Boolean, etc.) suggestions upon cell editing.
- Predefined attributes applicable to each column kind.

| Column | Description | Cells | Cell attributes | Column attributes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ColumnByFeatureView | Dedicated to meta-feature, or user-defined feature, value retrieval. It requires the expression to return only one meta-feature/user-defined feature, e.g., Element::declaredName or FeatureValue::value. The expression is specified for columnFeature.See procedure: on the page. on the page. | For ColumnByFeatureView, the cell type is calculated out of the meta-feature or user-defined feature and does not need to be specified explicitly. | maxDisplayedValues. It allows you to modify the default limit for displayed values in multi-valued table cells.Available values: Integer. If undefined or <=0, the attribute reverts to the default value.The default value is 10. >]]> | isMultiline. It allows you to specify whether single-valued string cells should be displayed in a multiline format.Available values: Boolean.The default value is false.pinning. It allows you to pin the column to the left or the right side of the table.Available values: left/right.By default, no column is pinned.sorting. It allows you to sort table rows in ascending or descending order according to the selected column. If the sorting is specified for multiple columns, use the sortingOrder attribute to specify the sorting order priority for the columns.Available values: asc/desc. If undefined or null, the column is not sorted.By default, the attribute value is undefined.sortingOrder. It allows you to specify the sorting order priority for the columns with the sorting attribute. The table rows are ordered for all columns based on the order specified using this attribute, e.g., a column with sortingOrder set to 0 will take the highest priority.If the sorting attribute is specified but the sortingOrder is not, sorting is applied without any order. Available values: Integer.By default, the attribute value is undefined.width. It allows you to specify the column width, where the integer value is interpreted in pixels. Available values: Integer. If undefined or null, the column width is responsive.By default, the attribute value is undefined.>]]> |
| ColumnByExpressionView | Dedicated to complex model queries. For example, when the value of the owned attribute needs to be retrieved, the feature chain expression is needed to access this information.See procedures: on the page. on the page. | For ColumnByExpressionView, the cell type is specified by defining the cell rendering with the appropriate rendering definition. It works as a kind of filter for the type of wanted expression results.The default cell rendering definition is StringCellRendering.Rendering definitionsrendering def BooleanCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def ElementCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def EnumCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def IntegerCellRendering :> TableCellRendering; rendering def MetaobjectCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def RealCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def StringCellRendering :> TableCellRendering;rendering def TableCellRendering { calc getValue { in rowElement [0..1] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; } attribute isMultiValued [0..1] : ScalarValues::Boolean; } |  |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">Tabular views contain columns <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a43b3e01-79af-434e-9dda-e1791f97df98">used to represent specific information about the exposed elements</ac:inline-comment-marker>. See the information on this page to learn about table columns before you start <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[modeling them]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Columns in tabular view definitions and usages</h3><p style="text-align: left;">Tabular views and their definitions can differ in the table columns they contain:</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="3ec94e1c-e1e5-45f5-93a9-452bcb101955">Tabular view definitions:<ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="cf4fd0b4-cf7c-4e77-a82b-9ae39821bac9"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Predefined tabular view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> come with predefined columns.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="a1e61253-c114-4172-9b26-27e4819b9ceb"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom tabular view definitions" /></ac:link> contain only the columns you specify. </li></ul></li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="e485e8a4-807d-4d11-9586-878b40e68064"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Tabular view usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> inherit columns from the view definitions they are defined by. You can also specify custom columns for them.</li></ul><h3 style="text-align: left;">Column kinds</h3><p style="text-align: left;">Currently, there are 2 model-based table column kinds available for modeling. The columns you create for a tabular view usage or definition must be defined by one of these column definitions, depending on the column's purpose. The table below lists:</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="5df54d9b-131e-4645-abd4-b5b1dc18380e">Column kinds and their descriptions.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="ba5b3521-0c18-45e8-b7e4-fb4b5ca0a431">Relevant information regarding cell type according to column kind.<br /><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="5d2bc526-c693-4cd4-b59e-066d811217b8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The cell type enables correct data representation and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="00a78496-3366-4701-b4cf-6b2f70e0c1bb">correct cell value type (e.g., String, Boolean, etc.) suggestions upon cell editing.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li></ul></li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="1defea6a-2f57-4371-9820-62e256f8c265">Predefined attributes applicable to each column kind.</li></ul><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Column</th><th scope="col">Description</th><th scope="col">Cells</th><th scope="col">Cell attributes</th><th scope="col">Column attributes</th></tr><tr><td><em>ColumnByFeatureView</em></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">Dedicated to meta-feature, or user-defined feature, value retrieval. It requires the expression to return only one meta-feature/user-defined feature, e.g., </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">Element::declaredName </ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">or </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">FeatureValue::value</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">. The expression is specified for </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">columnFeature</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f78726e-1408-4ff6-983c-46d998044c4a">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See procedure:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="1fff6c3d-49b7-4d56-9c62-19b962951df1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by feature"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding table columns by feature]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="09bb290a-0a47-45e3-9843-65e801abc755"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by feature"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding table columns by feature]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating custom tabular view definitions" /></ac:link> page.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></li></ul></td><td><ul><li data-uuid="250b3a27-0a06-4f19-84f9-e753e9ee9773"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>For <em>ColumnByFeatureView</em>, the cell type is calculated out of the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="250e72b1-5f1b-468a-8555-495793ba82f6"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a0670dd4-96e3-4cbe-8b1b-30ab4b63c96b">meta-feature or user-defined feature</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker> </span>and does not need to be specified explicitly.</span></span></li></ul></td><td rowspan="2"><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><em>maxDisplayedValues</em>. It allows you to modify the default limit for displayed values in multi-valued table cells.</span></span><ul><li data-uuid="b8f1362d-9a81-4f7c-a13e-2639e770d047"><span>Available values</span>: Integer. If undefined or &lt;=0, the attribute reverts to the default value.</li><li data-uuid="1838f934-d3fa-4c24-901b-dd0a4a2fe6ee"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The default value is 10. <br /><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying the value count in multi-valued cells"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See procedure >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li></ul></li></ul><div class="content-wrapper"><span> </span></div></td><td rowspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><span><em>isMultiline</em>. It allows you to specify whether single-valued string cells should be displayed in a multiline format.</span><ul><li data-uuid="bc29d39f-3592-4062-911f-fccc9f984e88"><span>Available values: Boolean.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="e5a1a894-050a-4aea-9e39-030b46fca172"><span>The default value is <em>false</em>.<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="050b80f8-0957-4889-a70c-e218a53a2d56"><span><em>pinning. </em>It allows you to pin the column to the left or the right side of the table.</span><ul><li data-uuid="464e3604-b298-42ce-bae4-9a7de24e0fea"><span>Available values: <em>left/right</em>.</span></li><li data-uuid="2ec08fba-92ec-40b7-913d-ea770e13a5a3"><span>By default, no column is pinned.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="8044834c-b16f-4910-aed0-925d09c8e144"><span><em>sorting. </em>It allows you to sort table rows in ascending or descending order according to the selected column. </span><ul><li data-uuid="47ee0e81-841b-4d04-998a-92fc618f606b"><span>If the sorting is specified for multiple columns, use the <em>sortingOrder </em>attribute to specify the sorting order priority for the columns.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="4d754b88-2e74-4f38-80ff-27472041ea45"><span>Available values: <em>asc/desc</em>. </span>If undefined or <em>null</em>, the column is not sorted.</li><li data-uuid="8044834c-b16f-4910-aed0-925d09c8e144"><span>By default, the attribute value is undefined.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="b142151b-568b-4e12-840a-f6cfee9f6126"><span><em>sortingOrder. </em>It allows you to specify the sorting order priority for the columns with the <em>sorting </em>attribute. The table rows are ordered for all columns based on the order specified using this attribute, e.g., a column with <em>sortingOrder </em>set to <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7ba171af-9fb8-46af-850c-44ef9e0f8a2e">0</ac:inline-comment-marker> will take the highest priority.</span><ul><li data-uuid="0b30f7da-bc77-4c06-9594-3ed99b9d2457"><span>If the <em>sorting </em>attribute is specified but the <em>sortingOrder </em>is not, <em>sorting </em>is applied without any order. </span></li><li data-uuid="fd67a89a-2785-4618-84f2-788ab3b58952"><span>Available values</span>: Integer.</li><li data-uuid="ac1efaa8-c5f9-4a64-b78c-1fca1beb6ed3"><span>By default, the attribute value is undefined.</span></li></ul></li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="b705a1a7-8a59-479e-8d58-a4b2bcd2450a"><span><em>width.</em> It allows you to specify the column width, where the integer value is interpreted in pixels. </span><ul><li data-uuid="dd35de50-396d-48f9-b703-4277309c7854"><span>Available values</span>: Integer. If undefined or <em>null</em>, the column width is responsive.</li><li data-uuid="6fc21655-e1f7-43b7-a323-d261ad48aa34"><span>By default, the attribute value is undefined.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying table column attributes"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[See procedure >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><em>ColumnByExpressionView</em></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Dedicated to complex model queries. For example, when the value of the owned attribute needs to be retrieved, the feature chain expression is needed to access this information.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See procedures:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="292872f5-f011-46ca-84d5-e60fb2cf0991"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by expression"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding table columns by expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li data-uuid="612bb176-65f0-4f54-a6a9-b898767aba86"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Adding table columns by expression"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating custom tabular view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Adding table columns by expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating custom tabular view definitions" /></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li data-uuid="3dec134c-fe7c-4ffb-8ebd-e3facc5f3402"><span>For <em>ColumnByExpressionView</em>, the cell type is specified by defining the cell rendering with the appropriate rendering definition. It works as a kind of filter for the type of <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2e4eec76-dcf8-425b-bad2-7d6ef344d3c5">wanted expression results</ac:inline-comment-marker>.<br /></span><ul><li data-uuid="1a979d84-132b-43f5-b781-82c63a6c75ae"><span>The default cell rendering definition is <em>StringCellRendering</em>.</span></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a4f5ac18-8795-4230-a5e5-cab99bbec49b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Rendering definitions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d29fa686-5aed-4181-8547-e15c52fcd897"><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">BooleanCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ElementCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">EnumCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">IntegerCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetaobjectCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">RealCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StringCellRendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>:&gt; TableCellRendering;<br /></span></p><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TableCellRendering</span><span> </span>{<br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">getValue</span><span> </span>{<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rowElement</span><span> </span>[<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] : Metaobjects::Metaobject; }<br />   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMultiValued</span><span> </span>[<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span>] : ScalarValues::Boolean; <br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577095 space=SYSML2P version=30 -->
## PAGE 00723: Tabular views

- page_id: `249577095`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577095/Tabular+views
- version_number: 30
- version_date: `2026-05-06T21:57:24.538+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Tabular views (i.e., tables) allow you to represent and modify information about the model elements and/or relationships between them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Tabular views

Specific tabular views you create in the modeling tool are tabular view usages defined by appropriate standard predefined tabular view definitions.

- For tabular view creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] page.
  - Additionally, you can create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom tabular view definitions' space=''] .
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Open in Table' space=''] functionality to view element content in a table without creating a view element in the model.

There are multiple predefined tabular view definitions available in the modeling tool, each designed for specific purposes:

| View definitions | Description | Columns | Availability |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Standard predefined tabular view definitions |  |  |  |
| Generic Table | Standard predefined multi-purpose generic tabular view for element representation and modeling. | Declared NameDeclared Short NameDocumentationQualified NameOwnerOwned Element | Via Textual EditorVia the Create View command |
| Requirements Table | Standard predefined tabular view for requirements representation and modeling.It subclassifies the NonStandardLibraryElementFilter view definition, which includes multiple filters that exclude various standard library elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. | Declared NameReq IDDocumentation |  |

##### Tabular view areas

Views are displayed in the View Editor tab.

- Table toolbar allows you to quickly access the options for managing data in the table.
  - See the page.
- Grid displays data according to the exposed elements and their filters.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in tabular views' space=''] page. The grid consists of:
  - Rows that appear along the side of the table, and are labeled using numbers starting with 1. Each table row represents an element .
    - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing tabular view rows' space=''] page.
  - Columns that appear along the top of the table, and are labeled according to the specified metadata feature or named by hand. Each table column represents specific element data.
    - You can also modify tabular view columns.
      - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling tabular view columns' space=''] page.
    - Column cells can be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling tabular view columns' space=''] .
  - Cells are the intersections of every row and column, which represent element data values.
    - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing tabular view cells' space=''] page.
- Table info area displays table information.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### Tabular view areas presented in a standard predefined tabular 'Generic Table' view.

###### Tabular view toolbar buttons

The table below shows the buttons available in the tabular view toolbar.

| Button | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Refresh | Click the button to update the displayed table content. |
|  | Open in Textual Editor | Click the button to open and highlight the table's textual notation in the Textual Editor. In a , the Textual Editor highlights all the content displayed in the table. |
|  | Add New | Clicking the button displays a dropdown menu for selecting an element to be created in the model and displayed in the table. >]]> |
|  | Delete | Clicking the button deletes the selected row elements from the table and the model. >]]> |
|  | Remove From Table | Clicking the button removes the selected row elements from the table, but not from the model. This is achieved by filtering out the selected element from the table. >]]>The button is unavailable in . |
|  | Columns | Clicking the button displays a dropdown menu for hiding/displaying table columns. It also contains a command for opening the Configure Table Columns dialog. >]]>The button is unavailable in . |
|  | Options | Clicking the button displays a dropdown menu for managing the table's display mode (e.g., Complete Tree, Compact Tree, List). >]]> |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Tabular views (i.e., tables) allow you to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">represent and modify information about the model elements and/or relationships between them. </span></p><p
````
